stem: On a q-sphere, a node (marker) is drawn at every computational basis state whose amplitude is nonzero. For the state below, which basis states appear as nodes? (Select 2.)
code:
from qiskit import QuantumCircuit
from qiskit.quantum_info import Statevector
from qiskit.visualization import plot_state_qsphere

qc = QuantumCircuit(2)
qc.h(0)
qc.x(1)
state = Statevector.from_instruction(qc)
fig = plot_state_qsphere(state)

explanation.correct: H puts qubit 0 into a superposition and X sets qubit 1 to |1>. In Qiskit's little-endian labels (q1 q0) the state is (|10> + |11>)/√2, so only |10> and |11> have nonzero amplitude and appear as q-sphere nodes. Node size encodes each amplitude's magnitude and node color its phase.

distractors.A: |00> requires qubit 1 to be |0>, but the X gate flipped qubit 1 to |1>, so |00> has zero amplitude and no node.
distractors.B: |01> also needs qubit 1 in |0>; since qubit 1 is |1>, this amplitude is zero and no node is drawn.
